title: After the Code
description: After the Calibrant's defeat. The code layer dissolves. Behind the terminal aesthetic: nothing. White. Empty. The party's stats begin to disappear. Each world stripped away a layer. What's left is just them. The step into World 6 is not a portal. It's just a step.

lines: Not crashed. Not deleted. Dissolved — the way a thought dissolves when the mind that was thinking it has moved on to something else. The green text stopped scrolling. The wireframe geometry lost its lines. The terminal aesthetic, which had been the entire architecture of this world, simply became transparent.
Not darkness. Nothing. The space that is not a space because the concept of space had not yet been defined there.
Not to zero. HP: gone. MP: gone. The interface that had floated above their heads in Node Prime, legible and literal, was dissolving along with everything else.

The Mage watched his damage formula vanish character by character. The Fighter's defense rating winked out. The Cleric's healing modifiers, gone. The Rogue's speed multiplier, gone. The Bard's buff stacks, gone.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: This is what it's been doing. The whole time. Every world stripped away a layer.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Medieval stripped away innocence. We learned that the world we started in was a design, not a home.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Suburban stripped away context. We learned our roles were costumes, not identities.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Steampunk stripped away craft. The precision and elegance of the Grand Mechanism was still in service of control. Beautiful things can serve ugly purposes.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Industrial stripped away meaning. Everything optimized, everything measured, nothing worth measuring for.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Digital stripped away the interface. The code was the last layer between us and what was actually being done to us.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: And now the interface is gone too.

steps.text: Nobody answered. The nothing beyond the dissolving terminal was very patient. It had, presumably, been waiting for them.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: That's what's left. When you take away the context and the costume and the craft and the meaning and the interface — what you can't strip away is the thing that was underneath all of it.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Us. Just us. All the way down.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Every world, the word was 'calibrated.' They were trying to calibrate us. All five of them. Whoever built these worlds wanted us calibrated.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: No. It didn't. That's why the interface is gone. There's nothing left for them to measure us with.

lines: There was no shimmer. No portal activating. No device humming to life in a parking lot or at the base of a clock tower or where a monitor used to be.
There was just the nothing. And in the nothing, the shape of a step forward.
The Bard went first this time. Because the Bard was the one who had named what they were, and naming things is the first act of presence in a space that has no name for anything.
The nothing accepted them the way nothing accepts everything: without condition, without comment, without calibration.
